source.title: Predicting pain: differential pain thresholds during self-induced, externally induced, and imagined self-induced pressure pain

confidence_rationale: Direct transfer of the efference-copy / forward-model logic from tactile to nociceptive input. Partial, graded attenuation is exactly the pattern SD-029 C2 specifies. Mapping_fidelity is high because the stream being attenuated is nociceptive, not tactile. Source_quality is strong (Pain is the flagship journal, pre-registered design, adequate power for within-subject contrasts).
failure_signatures: If SD-029's comparator residual were expected to produce binary extinction for self-caused harm, Lalouni's graded attenuation (self threshold ~40% higher than other-induced, with intermediate imagery condition) would falsify that expectation. Partial attenuation is the biologically correct signature.

The imagery condition attenuated pain even though no actual motor command was executed, suggesting the forward-model reference can be driven by an internally generated action prediction. This is a caveat for SD-029's strict reliance on a_actual as the conditioning signal -- if z_goal / z_intention can substitute in some conditions, the clean a_actual conditioning may be softer than the SD-029 spec implies.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Self-induced pressure pain thresholds are significantly higher than externally induced thresholds in healthy adults; imagined self-induction produces intermediate attenuation. Efference copies operate on nociceptive processing specifically, not only tactile.
mapping.ree_translation: This is the direct nociceptive-transfer evidence the SD-011 design doc flagged as missing. Lalouni 2020 establishes that the forward-model / efference-copy architecture SD-029 instantiates does operate on the nociceptive stream in biology. The attenuation is graded (self ~40% higher than other, imagery in between), matching SD-029 C2's 'partial attenuation, not binary gate' framing. The magnitude of attenuation sets a target for how large the residual difference between agent-caused and env-caused harm events should be in V3-EXQ-433a re-analysis.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Pressure pain on quadriceps in healthy adults via algometer is still not the V3 agent walking into a hazard tile in a gridworld -- the high-level structure transfers, but the timescale, sensorimotor coupling, and event density differ markedly. The imagery-condition attenuation also suggests the biological comparator is more flexible than SD-029's strict a_actual conditioning; this should be noted as an open boundary.
mapping.source_context: 40 healthy adults (18-35 years), pressure algometer applied to quadriceps femoris at 50 kPa/s. Three conditions (self, other, imagery), each tested three times, counterbalanced order. Mixed-effects model with by-subject random intercepts and slopes.

confidence_components.notes: Strong mapping to the nociceptive stream SD-029 targets. Transfer risk lower than Kilteni because modality matches, but some residual risk: healthy-adult static-pressure psychophysics vs V3 dynamic hazard-approach trajectories.
